config.heroHeadline: I want calmer stress response. What does the evidence actually support?
config.orientationHeading: Start by naming the stress pattern
config.orientationSubtext: The same ingredient can look useful or weak depending on acute pressure, baseline tension, reactivity, and sleep spillover.
config.safetyHeading: Stress-support decisions change when risk context changes
config.safetyBody: Do not use supplements to mask persistent panic symptoms, severe mood instability, unsafe medication interactions, or other complex clinical situations.

acute_stress.title: Acute stress
acute_stress.description: Short-term spikes in tension, pressure, or overwhelm.

stress_reactivity.title: Stress reactivity
stress_reactivity.description: Feeling easily triggered with stronger physical or emotional responses.

baseline_tension.title: Baseline tension
baseline_tension.description: Persistent background strain that does not fully reset.

stress_sleep_spillover.title: Stress-sleep spillover
stress_sleep_spillover.description: Stress carrying into bedtime, sleep continuity, or next-day recovery.

cognitive_stress_load.title: Cognitive stress load
cognitive_stress_load.description: Rumination, cognitive fatigue, or pressure-linked focus disruption.

claims.claim_statement: Ashwagandha is the most evidence-supported adaptogen for persistent background stress, with multiple RCTs showing cortisol reduction and validated stress-scale improvement.
claims.evidence_summary: Multiple RCTs using KSM-66 and Sensoril extracts report reductions in serum cortisol and validated stress-scale scores versus placebo in chronically stressed adults.
claims.limitations: Most trials are 8–12 weeks in populations selected for moderate-to-high baseline stress. Extract standardisation, dose, and preparation form vary across studies.
claims.best_fit: Persistent background stress in adults without contraindicated conditions, especially when cortisol context is relevant.
claims.not_best_fit: Thyroid conditions, autoimmune disease, pregnancy, breastfeeding, or concurrent sedative or immunosuppressant medication use.

claims.claim_statement: L-theanine is a plausible first-pass option for acute tension, with the most consistent signal around psychophysiological arousal during acute stress tasks.
claims.evidence_summary: Human crossover studies report reduced heart rate, cortisol, and subjective stress responses during controlled acute stress tasks versus placebo.
claims.limitations: Most studies use controlled lab stressors rather than real-world situations. Dose, timing, and caffeine co-administration vary; effects may not generalise to chronic high-stress contexts.
claims.best_fit: Short-term tension or pre-event stress where a non-sedating calming option is preferred and next-day heaviness is a concern.
claims.not_best_fit: Blood pressure medication contexts, persistent panic patterns, or expectations of a strong sedating effect.

claims.claim_statement: Rhodiola rosea is a candidate for stress-reactivity and burnout contexts where repeated stress demands are driving exhaustion and heightened response.
claims.evidence_summary: Human trials include signals on stress-related fatigue, burnout symptoms, and performance under load, but study populations and extract quality vary considerably.
claims.limitations: Trial heterogeneity, variability in extract composition, and lack of standardisation limit how broadly results generalise. Drug-herb interactions are possible.
claims.best_fit: Stress-related fatigue or burnout where reactivity and recovery capacity — rather than acute calming — are the primary concern.
claims.not_best_fit: Bipolar disorder, stimulant sensitivity, blood pressure management contexts, or expectations of an acute anti-anxiety effect.

claims.claim_statement: Magnesium is most decision-useful when stress is carrying into bedtime or next-day recovery, particularly where muscle tension or dietary insufficiency may be relevant.
claims.evidence_summary: Studies report associations between magnesium status, stress hormones, and sleep quality, with some RCTs showing benefit in older adults and high-stress populations.
claims.limitations: Many studies are observational or in specific populations such as older adults or athletes. Benefit may depend on baseline magnesium status rather than supplementation broadly.
claims.best_fit: Stress with nighttime tension, poor sleep continuity, or low dietary magnesium intake context.
claims.not_best_fit: Kidney disease, severe sleep disorders requiring medical evaluation, or expectations of a direct acute anxiolytic effect.

claims.claim_statement: Phosphatidylserine has the clearest fit when the stress pattern is cognitively loaded — sustained pressure, cortisol-driven focus disruption, or performance anxiety.
claims.evidence_summary: Human RCTs in exercise-stressed and cognitively loaded populations show cortisol blunting and cognitive performance retention under stress conditions.
claims.limitations: Most robust evidence is in exercise-stressed populations. Source (soy- vs bovine-derived), dose, and cognitive demand type matter. General office stress evidence is more limited.
claims.best_fit: Cognitive fatigue under sustained pressure, performance anxiety, or exercise-related stress where cortisol management is relevant.
claims.not_best_fit: Blood-thinning medication, shellfish or soy allergy depending on source, or general non-cognitively-loaded stress patterns.

safetyNotes.warning: Ashwagandha may elevate thyroid hormone levels and has been reported to exacerbate autoimmune conditions in some individuals.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Do not use self-directed with thyroid medications, diagnosed autoimmune disease, or immunosuppressants without clinician guidance.

safetyNotes.warning: Ashwagandha is contraindicated in pregnancy due to potential uterotonic effects and is not recommended during breastfeeding.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Stop or avoid use during pregnancy and breastfeeding.

safetyNotes.warning: Low blood pressure or antihypertensive medication can change the risk calculation for calming compounds.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Review medication context before using as an acute stress option.

safetyNotes.warning: Kidney disease changes magnesium handling and can make supplementation unsafe without clinician guidance.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Do not use magnesium as a self-directed stress experiment with kidney disease.

safetyNotes.warning: Phosphatidylserine may have mild anticoagulant properties; the interaction with blood-thinning medications is not well characterised.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Review anticoagulant or antiplatelet medication context before adding phosphatidylserine.

safetyNotes.warning: Rhodiola may interact with stimulants, antidepressants, and blood pressure medications; its mild activating effect can worsen agitation or insomnia in sensitive individuals.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Review current medications and start with a low dose; avoid late-day use if stimulant sensitivity is a concern.

ashwagandha-baseline-tension.title: Safety and efficacy of a high-concentration full-spectrum extract of ashwagandha root in reducing stress and anxiety in adults
ashwagandha-baseline-tension.source_note: KSM-66 RCT; cortisol reduction and perceived stress improvement in chronically stressed adults.

l-theanine-acute-stress.title: L-theanine reduces psychological and physiological stress responses
l-theanine-acute-stress.source_note: Human crossover study measuring heart rate and salivary IgA under an acute stress task; placebo-controlled.

rhodiola-stress-reactivity.title: A randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group study of the standardised extract SHR-5 of Rhodiola rosea in the treatment of subjects with stress-related fatigue
rhodiola-stress-reactivity.source_note: SHR-5 extract RCT; fatigue, burnout, and cognitive performance signals in stressed adults.

magnesium-stress-sleep-spillover.title: The Effect of Magnesium Supplementation on Subjective Anxiety and Stress: A Systematic Review
magnesium-stress-sleep-spillover.source_note: Systematic review; concludes modest benefit in stressed populations with possible baseline-status dependency.

phosphatidylserine-cognitive-stress.title: Effects of soy lecithin phosphatidic acid and phosphatidylserine complex (PAS) on the endocrine and psychological responses to mental stress
phosphatidylserine-cognitive-stress.source_note: RCT measuring cortisol and psychological responses to mental stress tasks; soy-derived PS complex.
